rationale: The $100,000 tax-liability protest threshold in §403.303(a) is a fixed dollar amount independent of the homestead exemption level. Raising the exemption reduces some owners' tax liabilities but does not render the protest statute inoperable or contradictory; no amendment is required.
quote: whose tax liability on the property is $100,000 or more may protest
_note: No direct conflict. Increasing the homestead exemption may reduce the number of property owners exceeding the $100,000 tax-liability threshold, but the statute remains operative without amendment.
